---
title: "Online Appendix for 'Macroeconomic Stabilization and Endogenous Inequality in a TANK Model with Dual Nominal Rigidities'"
---

This online appendix reports the nonlinear primitives of the model and the intermediate log-linearization steps underlying Section 3 of the main text. Appendix A in the main text derives the analytical reduction from the nonredundant 20-equation system to the five-equation canonical representation.

# Model Description

The appendix first states the nonlinear problems of the labor packer, households, firms, and government. It then collects a reduced nonlinear equilibrium system sufficient for the first-order approximation, and finally reports the nonredundant log-linear system used in Section 3 of the main text.

@tbl-nonlinear-variables summarizes the main variables and objects that appear before the log-linear approximation.

| Category | Notation | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Indices | $h\in[0,1]$ | Household or labor-variety index |
| Indices | $j\in[0,1]$ | Intermediate-good firm or goods-variety index |
| Types | $i\in\{H,S\}$ | Household type: hand-to-mouth ($H$) or saver ($S$) |
| Prices and wages | $P_{t}$ | Final-good price index |
| Prices and wages | $P_{t}(j)$ | Price set by intermediate-good firm $j$ |
| Prices and wages | $W^{N}_{t}$, $W^{N}_{t}(h)$ | Aggregate nominal wage and household-specific nominal wage |
| Prices and wages | $W^{N,i}_{t}$ | Type-$i$ nominal wage |
| Prices and wages | $W_{t}\equiv W^{N}_{t}/P_{t}$ | Aggregate real wage |
| Prices and wages | $W^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{N,i}_{t}/P_{t}$ | Type-$i$ real wage |
| Inflation rates | $\Pi^{p}_{t}\equiv P_{t}/P_{t-1}$ | Gross price inflation |
| Inflation rates | $\Pi^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{N,i}_{t}/W^{N,i}_{t-1}$ | Type-$i$ gross wage inflation |
| Inflation rates | $\Pi^{w}_{t}$ | Gross inflation of the aggregate wage index |
| Quantities | $C_{t}$, $C_{t}(h)$, $C^{i}_{t}$ | Aggregate, household-specific, and type-$i$ consumption |
| Quantities | $N_{t}$, $N_{t}(h)$, $N^{i}_{t}$ | Aggregate, household-specific, and type-$i$ labor |
| Quantities | $Y_{t}$, $Y_{t}(j)$ | Aggregate final output and output of intermediate-good firm $j$ |
| Preferences | $U(C,N)$ | Period utility function |
| Preferences | $U_{C}(C,N)$, $U_{N}(C,N)$ | Marginal utilities of consumption and labor |
| Preferences | $MRS(C,N)\equiv -U_{N}(C,N)/U_{C}(C,N)$ | Marginal rate of substitution |
| Technology | $A_{t}\equiv \exp(a_{t})$ | Aggregate productivity |
| Technology | $F(N)$, $F_{N}(N)$ | Production function and marginal product component |
| Technology | $MPN_{t}\equiv A_{t}F_{N}(N_{t})$ | Marginal product of labor |
| Markups | $MU^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{i}_{t}/MRS(C^{i}_{t},N^{i}_{t})$ | Type-$i$ wage markup |
| Markups | $MU^{w}_{t}\equiv W_{t}/MRS(C_{t},N_{t})$ | Aggregate wage markup |
| Markups | $MU^{p}_{t}\equiv MPN_{t}/W_{t}$ | Price markup |
| Assets and policy | $B^{N}_{t}(h)$, $B^{N}_{t}$ | Household and Type-S per-capita nominal bond holdings |
| Assets and policy | $V_{t}$, $\Omega_{t}(h)$ | Ex-dividend equity value and household equity holdings |
| Assets and policy | $Q_{t,t+i}$ | Saver stochastic discount factor from $t$ to $t+i$ |
| Assets and policy | $R^{N}_{t}$, $R_{t}$ | Gross nominal interest rate and ex ante gross real interest rate |
| Fiscal variables | $T^{H}_{t}$, $T^{S}_{t}$, $T^{M}_{t}$ | Net fiscal liabilities of Type H households, Type S households, and firms |
| Fiscal variables | $Z_{t}$, $Z$ | Transfer to Type H households and its steady-state component |
| Fiscal variables | $G_{t}$, $G$ | Government purchases and their steady-state component |
| Firms | $D_{t}(j)$, $D_{t}$ | Firm-$j$ and aggregate real dividends |
| Shocks | $a_{t}$, $m_{t}$, $z_{t}$, $g_{t}$ | Productivity, monetary-policy, redistribution, and government-spending shocks |
| Innovations | $e^{a}_{t}$, $e^{m}_{t}$, $e^{z}_{t}$, $e^{g}_{t}$ | Innovations to the exogenous processes |
| Multipliers | $\Lambda^{L}_{t}$, $\Lambda^{F}_{t}$, $\Lambda^{M}_{t}(j)$ | Multipliers for the labor packer, final-goods firm, and intermediate firm |
| Multipliers | $\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)$, $\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)$ | Type-H and Type-S household budget multipliers |

: Nonlinear variables and objects introduced before log-linearization {#tbl-nonlinear-variables tbl-colwidths="[22,28,50]"}

Throughout, upper-case letters denote level variables. The index $h\in[0,1]$ refers to households, and the index $j\in[0,1]$ refers to intermediate-goods firms. A superscript $i\in\{H,S\}$ indicates the household type, where ($H$) denotes hand-to-mouth households and $S$ denotes saver households. $P_{t}$ denotes the final-good price index, $W^{N}_{t}$ the aggregate nominal wage, and $W_{t}\equiv W^{N}_{t}/P_{t}$ the aggregate real wage. For each household type $i$, $C^{i}_{t}$, $N^{i}_{t}$, and $W^{N,i}_{t}$ denote consumption, labor supply, and the nominal wage, while $W^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{N,i}_{t}/P_{t}$ denotes the corresponding real wage. Aggregate price inflation is defined as $\Pi^{p}_{t}\equiv P_{t}/P_{t-1}$, and type-specific wage inflation is defined as $\Pi^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{N,i}_{t}/W^{N,i}_{t-1}$. Preferences are represented by a general period utility function $U(C,N)$, with marginal utilities $U_{C}(C,N)>0$ and $U_{N}(C,N)<0$. The marginal rate of substitution is $MRS(C,N)\equiv-U_{N}(C,N)/U_{C}(C,N)$. Intermediate firms use the general technology $Y=A_{t}F(N)$, with $F_{N}(N)>0$. The marginal product of labor is $MPN_{t}\equiv A_{t}F_{N}(N_{t})$. The type-$i$ wage markup is $MU^{i}_{t}\equiv W^{i}_{t}/MRS(C^{i}_{t},N^{i}_{t})$, and the price markup is $MU^{p}_{t}\equiv MPN_{t}/W_{t}$. Finally, $R^{N}_{t}$ denotes the gross nominal interest rate and $D_{t}$ firm dividends. The ex-dividend value of the aggregate equity claim is $V_t$, and $\Omega_t(h)$ denotes household $h$'s equity holdings. Saver households price claims with $Q_{t,t+i}\equiv\beta^i U_C(C^S_{t+i},N^S_{t+i})/U_C(C^S_t,N^S_t)$. Here $T^{i}_{t}$ denotes the net fiscal liability of type-$i$ households. A negative value therefore corresponds to a transfer received by that household type. The variable $Z_{t}$ denotes the transfer directed to Type H households. Government purchases are denoted by $G_{t}$, and $\Gamma\equiv G/Y$ is the steady-state government-spending share. In the redistribution rule considered below, the steady-state transfer is chosen so that type-specific steady-state consumption is equalized, $C^{H}=C^{S}$.

## Labor Packer

The labor packer is a competitive aggregator that combines differentiated household labor into a homogeneous labor input sold to intermediate-goods firms. Household $h$ supplies labor $N_{t}(h)$ and is paid the nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}(h)$, while the packer sells the aggregate labor input $N_{t}$ at the aggregate nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}$. The parameter $\psi_{w}>1$ is the elasticity of substitution across labor varieties, and $\Lambda^{L}_{t}$ is the multiplier on the constant-elasticity-of-substitution aggregation constraint. Taking the household-specific wages $\{W^{N}_{t}(h)\}_{h\in[0,1]}$ as given, the labor packer chooses $\{N_{t}(h)\}_{h\in[0,1]}$ and $N_{t}$, which yields the labor demand schedule for each household and the aggregate nominal wage index.

- Objects taken as given: $P_{t},W^{N}_{t},\{W^{N}_{t}(h)\}_{h\in[0,1]}$

- Choice variables: $N_{t},\{N_{t}(h)\}_{h\in[0,1]}$

- Objective Function: $$W^{N}_{t}N_{t}-\int^{1}_{0}W^{N}_{t}(h)N_{t}(h)dh$$

- Constraints: $$N_{t}=\left(\int^{1}_{0}N_{t}(h)^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}dh\right)^{\frac{\psi_{w}}{\psi_{w}-1}},\;\psi_{w}>1.$$

- Lagrangian: $$\begin{aligned}
  \mathcal{L}_{t}= & W^{N}_{t}N_{t}-\int^{1}_{0}W^{N}_{t}(h)N_{t}(h)dh\\
   & +\Lambda^{L}_{t}\left(\left(\int^{1}_{0}N_{t}(h)^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}dh\right)^{\frac{\psi_{w}}{\psi_{w}-1}}-N_{t}\right)
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $N_{t}$: $$W^{N}_{t}=\Lambda^{L}_{t}$$

- FOC wrt $N_{t}(h)$: $$N_{t}(h)=N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}$$

- Nominal wage: $$W^{N}_{t}=\left[\int^{1}_{0}W^{N}_{t}(h)^{1-\psi_{w}}dh\right]^{\frac{1}{1-\psi_{w}}}$$

## Households

There is a continuum of households indexed by $h\in[0,1]$. Each household consumes $C_{t}(h)$, supplies differentiated labor $N_{t}(h)$, and sets its own nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}(h)$. Preferences are common across households and are represented by period utility $U(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))$. Wage setting is subject to Rotemberg-type adjustment costs governed by $\eta_{w}$, and labor income is subsidized at rate $\tau_{w}$. A fraction $\lambda$ of households are hand-to-mouth households (Type H), who do not trade assets, while the remaining fraction $1-\lambda$ are saver households (Type S), who hold one-period nominal bonds and receive firm dividends. Within each type, full risk sharing implies identical consumption, labor, and wage choices in equilibrium.

With two household types and within-type symmetry, the labor-packer aggregation functions defined and derived in the previous subsection can be rewritten as follows:
$$\begin{aligned}
N_{t} & =\left[\lambda(N^{H}_{t})^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}+(1-\lambda)(N^{S}_{t})^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}\right]^{\frac{\psi_{w}}{\psi_{w}-1}}\\
W^{N}_{t} & =\left[\lambda(W^{N,H}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}+(1-\lambda)(W^{N,S}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}\right]^{\frac{1}{1-\psi_{w}}}\\
 & =P_{t}\left[\lambda(W^{H}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}+(1-\lambda)(W^{S}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}\right]^{\frac{1}{1-\psi_{w}}}
\end{aligned}$$

### Type H

A Type H household chooses current consumption $C_{t}(h)$, labor supply $N_{t}(h)$, and its own nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}(h)$, taking aggregate variables as given. Since it does not trade financial assets, its current uses of funds consist of consumption and the net fiscal liability $T^{H}_{t}$, where $T^{H}_{t}<0$ corresponds to a transfer received from the government. In the budget constraint, $P_{t}$ is the aggregate price level, $Y_{t}$ is aggregate output, and $\eta_{w}$ measures the cost of changing the nominal wage relative to the previous period's wage $W^{N}_{t-1}(h)$. The optimality conditions imply the marginal utility of consumption, the household-specific labor demand schedule, and the Type H wage-setting equation. In symmetric equilibrium, these conditions can be written in terms of the Type H real wage $W^{H}_{t}$, wage inflation $\Pi^{H}_{t}$, marginal rate of substitution $MRS^{H}_{t}\equiv MRS(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})$, and wage markup $MU^{H}_{t}\equiv W^{H}_{t}/MRS^{H}_{t}$.

- Objects taken as given: $W^{N}_{t},P_{t},N_{t},Y_{t},T^{H}_{t},W^{N}_{t-1}(h)$

- Choice variables: $C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h),W^{N}_{t}(h)$

- Objective Function: $$\mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}\beta^{t}U(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))$$

- Constraints: $$\begin{aligned}
   & C_{t}(h)+T^{H}_{t}\\
   & =(1+\tau_{w})\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{P_{t}}N_{t}(h)-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
   & N_{t}(h)=N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Lagrangian: $$\begin{aligned}
  \mathbb{L}= & \mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}\beta^{t}\mathcal{L}_{t}\\
  \mathcal{L}_{t}= & U\left(C_{t}(h),N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\right)\\
   & +\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)\left(\begin{array}{c}
  (1+\tau_{w})\frac{N_{t}}{P_{t}}W^{N}_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{1-\psi_{w}}-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
  -C_{t}(h)-T^{H}_{t}
  \end{array}\right)
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $C_{t}(h)$: $$\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)=U_{C}(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))$$

- FOC wrt $W^{N}_{t}(h)$: $$\begin{aligned}
  -\psi_{w}\frac{N_{t}}{W^{N}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}-1}U_{N}(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))\\
  +(1-\psi_{w})\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)(1+\tau_{w})\frac{N_{t}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  -\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)\eta_{w}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)\frac{Y_{t}}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}\\
  +\beta \mathbb{E}_{t}\Lambda^{H}_{t+1}(h)\eta_{w}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t+1}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}(h)}-1\right)\frac{W^{N}_{t+1}(h)Y_{t+1}}{W^{N}_{t}(h)^{2}} & =0
  \end{aligned}$$

- Symmetric Equilibrium ($\Lambda^{H}_{t}(h)=\Lambda^{H}_{t}$, $C_{t}(h)=C^{H}_{t}$, $N_{t}(h)=N^{H}_{t}$, $W^{N}_{t}(h)=W^{N,H}_{t}$): $$\begin{aligned}
  N^{H}_{t} & =N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  \Lambda^{H}_{t} & =U_{C}(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})
  \end{aligned}$$ $$\begin{aligned}
  \eta_{w}\frac{Y_{t}}{N^{H}_{t}}\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N,H}_{t-1}}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N,H}_{t-1}}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\left[MRS^{H}_{t}-(1+\tau_{w})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{w}}\right)\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{P_{t}}\right]\\
   & +\beta\eta_{w}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[\frac{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t+1},N^{H}_{t+1})}{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{N^{H}_{t}}\frac{W^{N,H}_{t+1}}{W^{N,H}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t+1}}{W^{N,H}_{t}}-1\right)\right]\\
  \Leftrightarrow\eta_{w}\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N,H}_{t-1}}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N,H}_{t-1}}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}N^{H}_{t}}{P_{t}Y_{t}}\left[\frac{1}{MU^{H}_{t}}-(1+\tau_{w})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{w}}\right)\right]\\
   & +\beta\eta_{w}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[\frac{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t+1},N^{H}_{t+1})}{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\frac{W^{N,H}_{t+1}}{W^{N,H}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t+1}}{W^{N,H}_{t}}-1\right)\right]
  \end{aligned}$$

### Type S

A Type S household chooses consumption $C_{t}(h)$, labor supply $N_{t}(h)$, its own nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}(h)$, one-period nominal bond holdings $B^{N}_{t}(h)$, and equity holdings $\Omega_{t}(h)$. In addition to current after-subsidy labor income, it receives gross nominal bond returns $R^{N}_{t-1}B^{N}_{t-1}(h)$ and the payoff on its equity holdings, $\Omega_{t-1}(h)(V_t+D_t)$, where $V_t$ is the ex-dividend value of the aggregate equity claim. It also faces the net fiscal liability $T^{S}_{t}$. A positive value of $T^{S}_{t}$ is therefore a levy, while a negative value corresponds to a transfer. Here $R^{N}_{t}$ denotes the gross nominal interest rate on the bond purchased in period $t$, and $B^{N}_{t}(h)/P_{t}$ is the household's real bond position. The optimality conditions deliver intertemporal asset-pricing equations and a wage-setting equation analogous to that of Type H households. In symmetric equilibrium, equity-market clearing gives $(1-\lambda)\Omega_t=1$, so Type S households receive the per-capita dividend $D_t/(1-\lambda)$.

- Objects taken as given: $W^{N}_{t},P_{t},R^{N}_{t},V_t,D_{t},N_{t},Y_{t},T^{S}_{t},W^{N}_{t-1}(h),B^{N}_{t-1}(h),\Omega_{t-1}(h)$

- Choice variables: $C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h),W^{N}_{t}(h),B^{N}_{t}(h),\Omega_t(h)$

- Objective Function: $$\mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}\beta^{t}U(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))$$

- Constraints: $$\begin{aligned}
   & C_{t}(h)+\frac{B^{N}_{t}(h)}{P_{t}}+\Omega_t(h)V_t+T^{S}_{t}\\
   & =(1+\tau_{w})\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{P_{t}}N_{t}(h)-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
   & +R^{N}_{t-1}\frac{B^{N}_{t-1}(h)}{P_{t}}+\Omega_{t-1}(h)(V_t+D_t)\\
   & N_{t}(h)=N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Lagrangian: $$\begin{aligned}
  \mathbb{L}= & \mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}\beta^{t}\mathcal{L}_{t}\\
  \mathcal{L}_{t}= & U\left(C_{t}(h),N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\right)\\
   & +\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)\left(\begin{array}{c}
  (1+\tau_{w})\frac{W^{N}_{t}N_{t}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{1-\psi_{w}}-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
  +R^{N}_{t-1}\frac{B^{N}_{t-1}(h)}{P_{t}}+\Omega_{t-1}(h)(V_t+D_t)-C_{t}(h)-\frac{B^{N}_{t}(h)}{P_{t}}-\Omega_t(h)V_t-T^{S}_{t}
  \end{array}\right)
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $C_{t}(h)$: $$\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)=U_{C}(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))$$

Define the saver stochastic discount factor from $t$ to $t+i$ as
$$Q_{t,t+i}\equiv\beta^i\frac{U_C(C^S_{t+i},N^S_{t+i})}{U_C(C^S_t,N^S_t)}.$$

- FOC wrt $W^{N}_{t}(h)$: $$\begin{aligned}
  -\psi_{w}\frac{N_{t}}{W^{N}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}-1}U_{N}(C_{t}(h),N_{t}(h))\\
  +(1-\psi_{w})\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)(1+\tau_{w})\frac{N_{t}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  -\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)\eta_{w}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t}(h)}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}-1\right)\frac{Y_{t}}{W^{N}_{t-1}(h)}\\
  +\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\eta_{w}\left(\frac{W^{N}_{t+1}(h)}{W^{N}_{t}(h)}-1\right)\frac{W^{N}_{t+1}(h)Y_{t+1}}{W^{N}_{t}(h)^{2}}\right] & =0
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $B^{N}_{t}(h)$: $$\frac{1}{P_t}=\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{R^N_t}{P_{t+1}}\right]$$

- FOC wrt $\Omega_t(h)$: $$V_t=\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}(V_{t+1}+D_{t+1})\right]$$

- Symmetric Equilibrium ($\Lambda^{S}_{t}(h)=\Lambda^{S}_{t}$, $C_{t}(h)=C^{S}_{t}$, $N_{t}(h)=N^{S}_{t}$, $W^{N}_{t}(h)=W^{N,S}_{t}$, where $W^{S}_{t}$ is the real wage of type $S$): $$\begin{aligned}
  N^{S}_{t} & =N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N}_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  \Lambda^{S}_{t} & =U_{C}(C^{S}_{t},N^{S}_{t})\\
  1= & \mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}R^{N}_{t}\frac{P_{t}}{P_{t+1}}\right]\\
  V_t= & \mathbb{E}_t\left[Q_{t,t+1}(V_{t+1}+D_{t+1})\right]\\
  = & \mathbb{E}_{t}\sum_{i=1}^{\infty}Q_{t,t+i}D_{t+i}
  \end{aligned}$$ $$\begin{aligned}
  \eta_{w}\frac{Y_{t}}{N^{S}_{t}}\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N,S}_{t-1}}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N,S}_{t-1}}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\left[MRS^{S}_{t}-(1+\tau_{w})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{w}}\right)\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{P_{t}}\right]\\
   & +\eta_{w}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{N^{S}_{t}}\frac{W^{N,S}_{t+1}}{W^{N,S}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t+1}}{W^{N,S}_{t}}-1\right)\right]\\
  \Leftrightarrow\eta_{w}\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N,S}_{t-1}}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N,S}_{t-1}}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}N^{S}_{t}}{P_{t}Y_{t}}\left[\frac{1}{MU^{S}_{t}}-(1+\tau_{w})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{w}}\right)\right]\\
   & +\eta_{w}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\frac{W^{N,S}_{t+1}}{W^{N,S}_{t}}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t+1}}{W^{N,S}_{t}}-1\right)\right]
  \end{aligned}$$ where $MRS^{S}_{t}=MRS(C^{S}_{t},N^{S}_{t})$ is Type S marginal rate of substitution, and $MU^{S}_{t}=W^{S}_{t}/MRS^{S}_{t}$ is Type S wage markup.

## Final Goods Firm

The final-goods firm is perfectly competitive and combines differentiated intermediate goods into a homogeneous final good using a constant-elasticity-of-substitution aggregator. The quantity of variety $j$ is denoted by $Y_{t}(j)$, its price by $P_{t}(j)$, aggregate final output by $Y_{t}$, and the final-good price index by $P_{t}$. The elasticity of substitution across intermediate varieties is $\psi_{p}\ge1$, and $\Lambda^{F}_{t}$ is the multiplier on the aggregation constraint. Taking the prices $\{P_{t}(j)\}_{j\in[0,1]}$ as given, the final-goods firm chooses ($\{Y_{t}(j)\}_{j\in[0,1]}$) and $Y_{t}$, which yields the demand schedule for each intermediate-good producer and the aggregate final-good price index.

- Objects taken as given: $P_{t},\{P_{t}(j)\}_{j\in[0,1]}$

- Choice variables: $Y_{t},\{Y_{t}(j)\}_{j\in[0,1]}$

- Objective Function: $$P_{t}Y_{t}-\int^{1}_{0}P_{t}(j)Y_{t}(j)dj$$

- Constraints: $$Y_{t}=\left(\int^{1}_{0}Y_{t}(j)^{\frac{\psi_{p}-1}{\psi_{p}}}dj\right)^{\frac{\psi_{p}}{\psi_{p}-1}},\;\psi_{p}\geq1$$

- Lagrangian: $$\begin{aligned}
  \mathcal{L}_{t}= & P_{t}Y_{t}-\int^{1}_{0}P_{t}(j)Y_{t}(j)dj\\
   & +\Lambda^{F}_{t}\left(\left(\int^{1}_{0}Y_{t}(j)^{\frac{\psi_{p}-1}{\psi_{p}}}dj\right)^{\frac{\psi_{p}}{\psi_{p}-1}}-Y_{t}\right)
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $Y_{t}$: $$\Lambda^{F}_{t}=P_{t}$$

- FOC wrt $Y_{t}(j)$: $$Y_{t}(j)=Y_{t}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{p}}$$

- Price of Final Goods: $$P_{t}=\left[\int^{1}_{0}P_{t}(j)^{1-\psi_{p}}dj\right]^{\frac{1}{1-\psi_{p}}}$$

## Intermediate Goods Firms

Intermediate-good firm $j$ produces output $Y_{t}(j)$ using labor $N_{t}(j)$ according to the general technology $Y_{t}(j)=A_{t}F(N_{t}(j))$, where $A_{t}\equiv\exp(a_{t})$ denotes aggregate labor productivity and $F_{N}(N)>0$. The firm hires homogeneous labor from the labor packer at the nominal wage $W^{N}_{t}$, sets its own price $P_{t}(j)$, and faces Rotemberg price adjustment costs governed by $\eta_{p}$. Its real profits are denoted by $D_{t}(j)$, the government levies the lump-sum tax $T^{M}_{t}$, and the sales subsidy rate is $\tau_{p}$. The firm's objective is the date-0 present discounted value of profits, discounted by the saver household's stochastic discount factor $Q_{0,t}$. The optimality conditions determine labor demand, real marginal cost, and the price-setting equation. In symmetric equilibrium, the price markup is $MU^{p}_{t}\equiv P_{t}MPN_{t}/W^{N}_{t}=MPN_{t}/W_{t}$ and $MPN_{t}\equiv A_{t}F_{N}(N_{t})$ is the marginal product of labor.

- Objects taken as given: $W^{N}_{t},P_{t},Y_{t},A_{t},T^{M}_{t},C^{S}_{t},P_{t-1}(j)$

- Choice variables: $N_{t}(j),P_{t}(j),Y_{t}(j)$

- Objective Function: $$\mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}Q_{0,t}D_{t}(j)$$

- Constraints: $$\begin{aligned}
  D_{t}(j) & =(1+\tau_{p})\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}Y_{t}(j)-\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t-1}(j)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}-\frac{W^{N}_{t}}{P_{t}}N_{t}(j)-T^{M}_{t}\\
  Y_{t}(j) & =Y_{t}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{p}}\\
  Y_{t}(j) & =A_{t}F(N_{t}(j))
  \end{aligned}$$

- Lagrangian: $$\begin{aligned}
  \mathbb{L}= & \mathbb{E}_{0}\sum^{\infty}_{t=0}Q_{0,t}\mathcal{L}_{t}\\
  \mathcal{L}_{t}= & (1+\tau_{p})Y_{t}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{1-\psi_{p}}-\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t-1}(j)}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}-\frac{W^{N}_{t}}{P_{t}}N_{t}(j)\\
   & -T^{M}_{t}+\Lambda^{M}_{t}(j)\left(A_{t}F(N_{t}(j))-Y_{t}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{p}}\right)
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $N_{t}(j)$: $$\begin{aligned}
  \Lambda^{M}_{t}(j)A_{t}F_{N}(N_{t}(j)) & =W^{N}_{t}/P_{t}
  \end{aligned}$$

- FOC wrt $P_{t}(j)$: $$\begin{aligned}
   & Q_{0,t}(1+\tau_{p})(1-\psi_{p})\frac{Y_{t}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{p}}-Q_{0,t}\eta_{p}\frac{Y_{t}}{P_{t-1}(j)}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t-1}(j)}-1\right)\\
   & +Q_{0,t}\psi_{p}\Lambda^{M}_{t}(j)\frac{Y_{t}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{P_{t}(j)}{P_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{p}-1}\\
   & +\eta_{p}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{0,t+1}Y_{t+1}\frac{P_{t+1}(j)}{P_{t}(j)^{2}}\left(\frac{P_{t+1}(j)}{P_{t}(j)}-1\right)\right]=0
  \end{aligned}$$

- Symmetric Equilibrium ($N_{t}(j)=N_{t}$, $\Lambda^{M}_{t}(j)=\Lambda^{M}_{t}$, $P_{t}(j)=P_{t}$, $Y_{t}(j)=Y_{t}$): $$\begin{aligned}
  \eta_{p}\frac{P_{t}}{P_{t-1}}\left(\frac{P_{t}}{P_{t-1}}-1\right)= & \psi_{p}\left[\frac{W^{N}_{t}/P_{t}}{MPN_{t}}-(1+\tau_{p})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{p}}\right)\right]\\
   & +\eta_{p}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\frac{P_{t+1}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{P_{t+1}}{P_{t}}-1\right)\right]\\
  = & \psi_{p}\left[\frac{1}{MU^{p}_{t}}-(1+\tau_{p})\left(1-\frac{1}{\psi_{p}}\right)\right]\\
   & +\eta_{p}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\frac{P_{t+1}}{P_{t}}\left(\frac{P_{t+1}}{P_{t}}-1\right)\right]
  \end{aligned}$$

- Profits: $$\begin{aligned}
  D_{t} & =(1+\tau_{p})Y_{t}-\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}\left(\frac{P_{t}}{P_{t-1}}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}-\frac{W^{N}_{t}}{P_{t}}N_{t}-T^{M}_{t}
  \end{aligned}$$ When $(1+\tau_{p})(1-1/\psi_{p})=1$, steady state price markup is one.

## Government

The government performs three functions in the model. First, it provides the wage subsidy $\tau_{w}$ and the sales subsidy $\tau_{p}$, eliminating steady-state markups under the baseline specialization introduced below. Second, it redistributes resources across household types through the lump-sum components $T^{H}_{t}$ and $T^{S}_{t}$, and finances government purchases $G_{t}$ with type-invariant lump-sum liabilities. Here $Z_{t}$ denotes the transfer received by Type H households, while Type S households finance that transfer through a balanced-budget levy. Because $T^{i}_{t}$ denotes net fiscal liability, the transfer implies $T^{H}_{t}<0$ for the recipient household type and a positive levy on Type S through $T^{S}_{t}$. The steady-state component of the transfer is chosen to equalize type-specific consumption in the steady state. Third, the monetary authority sets the gross nominal interest rate $R^{N}_{t}$ according to a Taylor rule that responds to gross price inflation $\Pi^{p}_{t}$ and to the monetary policy shock $m_{t}$.

Fiscal policy: $$\begin{aligned}
T^{H}_{t} & =-Z_{t}+\tau_{w}W^{H}_{t}N^{H}_{t}+G_{t}\\
T^{S}_{t} & =\frac{\lambda}{1-\lambda}Z_{t}+\tau_{w}W^{S}_{t}N^{S}_{t}+G_{t}\\
T^{M}_{t} & =\tau_{p}Y_{t}\\
\tau_{w} & =1/(\psi_{w}-1)\\
\tau_{p} & =1/(\psi_{p}-1)\\
Z_{t} & =Z+z_{t}Y\\
Z & =D\\
C^{H} & =C^{S}\\
z_{t} & =\rho_{z}z_{t-1}+e^{z}_{t}\\
G_{t} & =G+g_{t}Y\\
\Gamma & =G/Y\\
g_{t} & =\rho_{g}g_{t-1}+e^{g}_{t}
\end{aligned}$$

Monetary Policy:

$$\begin{aligned}
R^{N}_{t} & =(1/\beta)\exp(-m_{t})(\Pi^{p}_{t})^{\phi}\\
m_{t} & =\rho_{m}m_{t-1}+e^{m}_{t}
\end{aligned}$$

## Equilibrium conditions

Equilibrium requires market clearing in goods, bond, and equity markets, together with consistency between aggregate variables and type-specific allocations. Let $B^N_t$ denote Type-S per-capita nominal bond holdings in symmetric equilibrium. Bond-market clearing requires zero net bond supply, so $(1-\lambda)B^{N}_{t}=0$. Equity supply is normalized to one, so equity-market clearing requires $(1-\lambda)\Omega_t=1$. Aggregate consumption $C_{t}$ is the population-weighted average of $C^{H}_{t}$ and $C^{S}_{t}$, and final output $Y_{t}$ is allocated to private consumption, government purchases, and price and wage adjustment costs.

Final goods market: $$\begin{aligned}
Y_{t} & =C_{t}+G_{t}+\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}\left(\frac{P_{t}}{P_{t-1}}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
 & +\lambda\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N,H}_{t}}{W^{N,H}_{t-1}}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}+(1-\lambda)\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\frac{W^{N,S}_{t}}{W^{N,S}_{t-1}}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}
\end{aligned}$$

Bond market: $(1-\lambda)B^{N}_{t}=0$.

Equity market: $(1-\lambda)\Omega_t=1$.

Aggregation condition: $$C_{t}=\lambda C^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)C^{S}_{t}$$

## Reduced nonlinear equilibrium system

Before summarizing the model, I collect the key real variables and inflation rates used below. In particular, $W_{t}\equiv W^{N}_{t}/P_{t}$ denotes the aggregate real wage, $W^{H}_{t}\equiv W^{N,H}_{t}/P_{t}$ and $W^{S}_{t}\equiv W^{N,S}_{t}/P_{t}$ denote type-specific real wages, $\Pi^{p}_{t}\equiv P_{t}/P_{t-1}$ denotes gross price inflation, and $\Pi^{H}_{t}$, $\Pi^{S}_{t}$, and $\Pi^{w}_{t}$ denote the corresponding gross wage inflation rates for Type H, Type S, and the aggregate wage index. The aggregate wage markup is defined as $MU^{w}_{t}\equiv W_{t}/MRS(C_{t},N_{t})$. The saver stochastic discount factor is $Q_{t,t+i}\equiv\beta^iU_C(C^S_{t+i},N^S_{t+i})/U_C(C^S_t,N^S_t)$.

The following block collects a reduced set of nonlinear equilibrium conditions that is sufficient for the log-linearization below.

I report only the nonlinear conditions needed to obtain the first-order system below. Conditions omitted here are either redundant or recoverable by symmetry and aggregation.

The nonlinear equilibrium conditions can be summarized as follows:

- Labor Packer: $$\begin{aligned}
  W_{t} & =\left[\lambda(W^{H}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}+(1-\lambda)(W^{S}_{t})^{1-\psi_{w}}\right]^{\frac{1}{1-\psi_{w}}}\\
  N_{t} & =\left[\lambda(N^{H}_{t})^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}+(1-\lambda)(N^{S}_{t})^{\frac{\psi_{w}-1}{\psi_{w}}}\right]^{\frac{\psi_{w}}{\psi_{w}-1}}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Type H: $$\begin{aligned}
  N^{H}_{t}= & N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{H}_{t}}{W_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  C^{H}_{t}= & W^{H}_{t}N^{H}_{t}+Z_{t}-G_{t}-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\Pi^{H}_{t}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
  \eta_{w}\Pi^{H}_{t}\left(\Pi^{H}_{t}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\left\{ \frac{1}{MU^{H}_{t}}-1\right\} \frac{W^{H}_{t}N^{H}_{t}}{Y_{t}}\\
   & +\eta_{w}\beta\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[\frac{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t+1},N^{H}_{t+1})}{U_{C}(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\Pi^{H}_{t+1}\left(\Pi^{H}_{t+1}-1\right)\right]\\
  \Pi^{H}_{t}= & \frac{W^{H}_{t}}{W^{H}_{t-1}}\Pi^{p}_{t}\\
  MU^{H}_{t}= & \frac{W^{H}_{t}}{MRS(C^{H}_{t},N^{H}_{t})}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Type S: $$\begin{aligned}
  N^{S}_{t}= & N_{t}\left(\frac{W^{S}_{t}}{W_{t}}\right)^{-\psi_{w}}\\
  Q_{t,t+i}= & \beta^i\frac{U_C(C^S_{t+i},N^S_{t+i})}{U_C(C^S_t,N^S_t)}\\
  1= & \mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{R^{N}_{t}}{\Pi^{p}_{t+1}}\right]\\
  V_t= & \mathbb{E}_t\left[Q_{t,t+1}(V_{t+1}+D_{t+1})\right]=\mathbb{E}_{t}\sum_{i=1}^{\infty}Q_{t,t+i}D_{t+i}\\
  C^{S}_{t}= & W^{S}_{t}N^{S}_{t}+\frac{D_{t}}{1-\lambda}-\frac{\lambda}{1-\lambda}Z_{t}-G_{t}-\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}\left(\Pi^{S}_{t}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}\\
  \eta_{w}\Pi^{S}_{t}\left(\Pi^{S}_{t}-1\right)= & \psi_{w}\left\{ \frac{1}{MU^{S}_{t}}-1\right\} \frac{W^{S}_{t}N^{S}_{t}}{Y_{t}}\\
   & +\eta_{w}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\Pi^{S}_{t+1}\left(\Pi^{S}_{t+1}-1\right)\right]\\
  \Pi^{S}_{t}= & \frac{W^{S}_{t}}{W^{S}_{t-1}}\Pi^{p}_{t}\\
  MU^{S}_{t}= & \frac{W^{S}_{t}}{MRS(C^{S}_{t},N^{S}_{t})}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Firms: $$\begin{aligned}
  A_{t} & =\exp(a_{t})\\
  Y_{t} & =A_{t}F(N_{t})\\
  a_{t} & =\rho_{a}a_{t-1}+e^{a}_{t}\\
  \eta_{p}\Pi^{p}_{t}\left(\Pi^{p}_{t}-1\right) & =\psi_{p}\left\{ \frac{1}{MU^{p}_{t}}-1\right\} \\
   & +\eta_{p}\mathbb{E}_{t}\left[Q_{t,t+1}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\Pi^{p}_{t+1}\left(\Pi^{p}_{t+1}-1\right)\right]\\
  MU^{p}_{t} & =\frac{A_{t}F_{N}(N_{t})}{W_{t}}\\
  D_{t} & =Y_{t}-\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}\left(\Pi^{p}_{t}-1\right)^{2}Y_{t}-W_{t}N_{t}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Government: $$\begin{aligned}
  R^{N}_{t} & =\frac{\exp(-m_{t})}{\beta}(\Pi^{p}_{t})^{\phi}\\
  m_{t} & =\rho_{m}m_{t-1}+e^{m}_{t}\\
  Z_{t} & =Z+z_{t}Y\\
  Z & =D\\
  G_{t} & =G+g_{t}Y\\
  \Gamma & =G/Y\\
  z_{t} & =\rho_{z}z_{t-1}+e^{z}_{t}\\
  g_{t} & =\rho_{g}g_{t-1}+e^{g}_{t}\\
  \end{aligned}$$

- Market clearing and others:

$$\begin{aligned}
Y_{t}= & C_{t}+G_{t}+\frac{\eta_{p}}{2}(\Pi^{p}_{t}-1)^{2}Y_{t}\\
 & +\lambda\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}(\Pi^{H}_{t}-1)^{2}Y_{t}+(1-\lambda)\frac{\eta_{w}}{2}(\Pi^{S}_{t}-1)^{2}Y_{t}\\
C_{t}= & \lambda C^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)C^{S}_{t}\\
(1-\lambda)B^N_t= & 0\\
(1-\lambda)\Omega_t= & 1\\
R_{t}= & \mathbb{E}_{t}\left[\frac{R^{N}_{t}}{\Pi^{p}_{t+1}}\right]\\
\Pi^{w}_{t}= & \frac{W_{t}}{W_{t-1}}\Pi^{p}_{t}\\
MU^{w}_{t}= & \frac{W_{t}}{MRS(C_{t},N_{t})}
\end{aligned}$$

## Functional specialization and log-linear approximation

From this section onward, I specialize the primitives to the separable CRRA-Frisch utility function and set $F(N)=N^{1-\alpha}$:
$$\begin{aligned}
U(C,N) & =\frac{C^{1-\gamma}}{1-\gamma}-\frac{N^{1+\varphi}}{1+\varphi},\\
MRS(C,N) & =C^{\gamma}N^{\varphi},\\
F(N) & =N^{1-\alpha},\\
F_{N}(N) & =(1-\alpha)N^{-\alpha}.
\end{aligned}$$

In the log-linearized system, lower-case letters denote log deviations from the symmetric zero-inflation steady state; for example, $c_{t}\equiv\log(C_{t}/C)$, $n_{t}\equiv\log(N_{t}/N)$, and $w_{t}\equiv\log(W_{t}/W)$. Inflation variables are log deviations of gross inflation rates. The dividend variable is the deviation of dividends from their steady-state value, scaled by steady-state output, $d_{t}\equiv(D_{t}-D)/Y$. The ex ante real interest rate is $r_{t}\equiv r^{N}_{t}-\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1}$, where $r^{N}_{t}$ is the log deviation of the gross nominal rate $R^{N}_{t}$. The variables $a_{t}$, $m_{t}$, $z_{t}\equiv(Z_{t}-Z)/Y$, and $g_{t}\equiv(G_{t}-G)/Y$ are exogenous deviation processes. In what follows, upper-case $MU$ denotes level markups, whereas lower-case $\mu$ denotes their log-linear deviations. Around the symmetric zero-inflation steady state, the quadratic price- and wage-adjustment costs are second-order terms and therefore drop out from the first-order resource constraint, which implies $y_{t}=(1-\Gamma)c_{t}+g_{t}$ at first order. The steady-state labor share remains $WN/Y=1-\alpha$.

The steady-state restrictions used below are as follows: $$\begin{aligned}
N & =N^{S}=N^{H}=\left(\frac{1-\alpha}{(1-\Gamma)^{\gamma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\gamma(1-\alpha)+\varphi+\alpha}}\\
Y & =N^{1-\alpha}\\
C&=C^{S}=C^{H}=(1-\Gamma)Y\\
W & =W^{S}=W^{H}=(1-\alpha)N^{-\alpha}\\
MU^p &=MU^{w}= MU^{S}=MU^{H}=1\\
\Pi^{p}&=\Pi^{w}=  \Pi^{S}=\Pi^{H}=1\\
D/Y&=  \alpha\\
G/Y&= \Gamma\\
V/Y&= \frac{\beta}{1-\beta}\alpha\\
\Omega&= \frac{1}{1-\lambda}
\end{aligned}$$

The log-linear approximation, with $d_{t}:=(D_{t}-D)/Y$, is then given by:

- Labor Packer: $$\begin{aligned}
  w_{t} & =\lambda w^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)w^{S}_{t}\\
  n_{t} & =\lambda n^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)n^{S}_{t}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Type H: $$\begin{aligned}
  n^{H}_{t} & =n_{t}-\psi_{w}(w^{H}_{t}-w_{t})\\
  c^{H}_{t} & =\frac{1-\alpha}{1-\Gamma}(w^{H}_{t}+n^{H}_{t})+\frac{1}{1-\Gamma}(z_{t}-g_{t})\\
  \pi^{H}_{t} & =-(1-\alpha)(\psi_{w}/\eta_{w})\mu^{H}_{t}+\beta\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{H}_{t+1}\\
  \pi^{H}_{t} & =w^{H}_{t}-w^{H}_{t-1}+\pi^{p}_{t}\\
  \mu^{H}_{t} & =w^{H}_{t}-(\gamma c^{H}_{t}+\varphi n^{H}_{t})\\
  \end{aligned}$$

- Type S: $$\begin{aligned}
  n^{S}_{t} & =n_{t}-\psi_{w}(w^{S}_{t}-w_{t})\\
  r^{N}_{t}-\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1} & =\gamma\mathbb{E}_{t}\Delta c^{S}_{t+1}\\
  c^{S}_{t} & =\frac{1-\alpha}{1-\Gamma}(w^{S}_{t}+n^{S}_{t})+\frac{1}{(1-\lambda)(1-\Gamma)}d_{t}-\frac{\lambda}{(1-\lambda)(1-\Gamma)}z_{t}-\frac{1}{1-\Gamma}g_{t}\\
  \pi^{S}_{t} & =-(1-\alpha)(\psi_{w}/\eta_{w})\mu^{S}_{t}+\beta\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{S}_{t+1}\\
  \pi^{S}_{t} & =w^{S}_{t}-w^{S}_{t-1}+\pi^{p}_{t}\\
  \mu^{S}_{t} & =w^{S}_{t}-(\gamma c^{S}_{t}+\varphi n^{S}_{t})
  \end{aligned}$$

- Firms: $$\begin{aligned}
  y_{t} & =a_{t}+(1-\alpha)n_{t}\\
  a_{t} & =\rho_{a}a_{t-1}+e^{a}_{t}\\
  \pi^{p}_{t} & =-(\psi_{p}/\eta_{p})\mu^{p}_{t}+\beta\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1}\\
  \mu^{p}_{t} & =y_{t}-n_{t}-w_{t}\\
  d_{t} & =y_{t}-(1-\alpha)(w_{t}+n_{t})
  \end{aligned}$$

- Government: $$\begin{aligned}
  r^{N}_{t} & =\phi\pi^{p}_{t}-m_{t}\\
  m_{t} & =\rho_{m}m_{t-1}+e^{m}_{t}\\
  z_{t} & =\rho_{z}z_{t-1}+e^{z}_{t}\\
  g_{t} & =\rho_{g}g_{t-1}+e^{g}_{t}
  \end{aligned}$$

- Market clearing and others:

$$\begin{aligned}
y_{t} & =(1-\Gamma)c_{t}+g_{t}\\
r_{t} & =r^{N}_{t}-\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1}\\
c_{t} & =\lambda c^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)c^{S}_{t}\\
\pi^{w}_{t} & =\lambda\pi^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)\pi^{S}_{t}\\
\pi^{w}_{t} & =w_{t}-w_{t-1}+\pi^{p}_{t}\\
\pi^{w}_{t} & =-(1-\alpha)(\psi_{w}/\eta_{w})\mu^{w}_{t}+\beta\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{w}_{t+1}\\
\mu^{w}_{t} & =w_{t}-(\gamma c_{t}+\varphi n_{t})
\end{aligned}$$

After eliminating the nominal rate $r^{N}_{t}$, @tbl-nonredundant-20eq reports the extended first-order representation. Relative to the baseline system, the table uses the dividend gap $d_{t}\equiv(D_{t}-D)/Y$, includes the government-spending deviation $g_{t}\equiv(G_{t}-G)/Y$, and keeps the steady-state labor share fixed at $1-\alpha$.

| No. | Name | Equation |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 1 | Type S Euler equation | $r_{t}=\gamma \mathbb{E}_{t}\Delta c^{S}_{t+1}$ |
| 2 | Type H budget constraint | $c^{H}_{t}=\frac{1-\alpha}{1-\Gamma}(w^{H}_{t}+n^{H}_{t})+\frac{1}{1-\Gamma}(z_{t}-g_{t})$ |
| 3 | Aggregate consumption aggregation identity | $c_{t}=\lambda c^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)c^{S}_{t}$ |
| 4 | Aggregate labor aggregation identity | $n_{t}=\lambda n^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)n^{S}_{t}$ |
| 5 | Aggregate wage Phillips curve | $\pi^{w}_{t}=-(1-\alpha)(\psi_{w}/\eta_{w})\mu^{w}_{t}+\beta \mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{w}_{t+1}$ |
| 6 | Aggregate wage-markup definition | $\mu^{w}_{t}=w_{t}-\gamma c_{t}-\varphi n_{t}$ |
| 7 | Aggregate wage-inflation identity | $\pi^{w}_{t}=w_{t}-w_{t-1}+\pi^{p}_{t}$ |
| 8 | Type H wage Phillips curve | $\pi^{H}_{t}=-(1-\alpha)(\psi_{w}/\eta_{w})\mu^{H}_{t}+\beta \mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{H}_{t+1}$ |
| 9 | Type H wage-markup definition | $\mu^{H}_{t}=w^{H}_{t}-\gamma c^{H}_{t}-\varphi n^{H}_{t}$ |
| 10 | Type H wage-inflation identity | $\pi^{H}_{t}=w^{H}_{t}-w^{H}_{t-1}+\pi^{p}_{t}$ |
| 11 | Type H labor demand | $n^{H}_{t}=n_{t}-\psi_{w}(w^{H}_{t}-w_{t})$ |
| 12 | Aggregate wage aggregation identity | $w_{t}=\lambda w^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)w^{S}_{t}$ |
| 13 | Aggregate wage-markup aggregation identity | $\mu^{w}_{t}=\lambda \mu^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)\mu^{S}_{t}$ |
| 14 | Aggregate wage-inflation aggregation identity | $\pi^{w}_{t}=\lambda\pi^{H}_{t}+(1-\lambda)\pi^{S}_{t}$ |
| 15 | Price Phillips curve | $\pi^{p}_{t}=-(\psi_{p}/\eta_{p})\mu^{p}_{t}+\beta \mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1}$ |
| 16 | Price-markup definition | $\mu^{p}_{t}=y_{t}-n_{t}-w_{t}$ |
| 17 | Dividend gap | $d_{t}=y_{t}-(1-\alpha)(w_{t}+n_{t})$ |
| 18 | Resource constraint | $y_{t}=(1-\Gamma)c_{t}+g_{t}$ |
| 19 | Production function | $y_{t}=a_{t}+(1-\alpha)n_{t}$ |
| 20 | Taylor rule | $r_{t}=\phi\pi^{p}_{t}-\mathbb{E}_{t}\pi^{p}_{t+1}-m_{t}$ |

: Extended first-order representation {#tbl-nonredundant-20eq tbl-colwidths="[5,52,43]"}

For convenience, @tbl-variables lists the endogenous variables appearing in @tbl-nonredundant-20eq, and @tbl-parameters reports the core structural parameters used below.

| No. | Meaning | Notation |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 1 | Output | $y_{t}$ |
| 2 | Aggregate consumption | $c_{t}$ |
| 3 | Aggregate labor | $n_{t}$ |
| 4 | Aggregate real wage | $w_{t}$ |
| 5 | Dividend gap | $d_{t}$ |
| 6 | Ex ante real interest rate | $r_{t}$ |
| 7 | Price markup | $\mu^{p}_{t}$ |
| 8 | Price inflation | $\pi^{p}_{t}$ |
| 9 | Aggregate wage markup | $\mu^{w}_{t}$ |
| 10 | Aggregate wage inflation | $\pi^{w}_{t}$ |
| 11 | Type H consumption | $c^{H}_{t}$ |
| 12 | Type S consumption | $c^{S}_{t}$ |
| 13 | Type H labor | $n^{H}_{t}$ |
| 14 | Type S labor | $n^{S}_{t}$ |
| 15 | Type H real wage | $w^{H}_{t}$ |
| 16 | Type S real wage | $w^{S}_{t}$ |
| 17 | Type H wage markup | $\mu^{H}_{t}$ |
| 18 | Type S wage markup | $\mu^{S}_{t}$ |
| 19 | Type H wage inflation | $\pi^{H}_{t}$ |
| 20 | Type S wage inflation | $\pi^{S}_{t}$ |

: Endogenous variables in the nonredundant system {#tbl-variables tbl-colwidths="[6,72,22]"}

| No. | Meaning | Parameter |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 1 | Inverse of elasticity of intertemporal substitution | $\gamma>0$ |
| 2 | Inverse of Frisch elasticity | $\varphi>0$ |
| 3 | Discount factor | $0<\beta<1$ |
| 4 | Elasticity of substitution across intermediate-goods varieties | $\psi_{p}\geq1$ |
| 5 | Price adjustment cost | $\eta_{p}\geq0$ |
| 6 | Elasticity of substitution across labor varieties | $\psi_{w}\geq1$ |
| 7 | Wage adjustment cost | $\eta_{w}\geq0$ |
| 8 | Taylor-rule coefficient on price inflation | $\phi>1$ |
| 9 | Share of Type H households | $0\leq\lambda<1$ |
| 10 | Production curvature | $0\leq\alpha<1$ |
| 11 | Steady-state government-spending share | $0\leq\Gamma<1$ |

: Core structural parameters {#tbl-parameters tbl-colwidths="[6,72,22]"}
